text: Robotic right adrenalectomy for metastatic sarcoma. Seventy-three-year-old woman presented with history of right upper extremity pleuromorphic sarcoma, which had been resected in the past. He was found to have three centimeter right adrenal mass on the PET scan and MRI with increased SUV uptake. On the MRI, you can see there is a large lesion in the right adrenal gland.

The surgeon uses a five-port technique with the robotic system to elevate the right hepatic lobe cephalad. Adhesions between the inferior aspect of the liver and the right reticulum plutonium are dissected using robotic instruments, including a bipolar device. Robotic arm four lifts the right hemiliver with a bowel grasper, ensuring atraumatic manipulation. The right coronary and triangular ligaments are also dissected, with care taken to avoid injury to the right hemidiaphragm during liver mobilization.

The assistant uses a laparoscopic suction device to evacuate smoke and restrict the kidney posteriorly. The right hepatic lobe is mobilized towards the lateral wall of the vena cava, which is visible on the screen. The gallbladder is elevated cephalad and resected robotically to access the area behind the liver. The cystic duct is clipped and transected, and the gallbladder is placed in a laparoscopic endobag for removal from the abdomen.

The surgeon uses robotic arm four with a bowel grasper to expose the surgical area, gradually taking down the right coronary and triangle ligaments with medial retraction. The lesion is encountered as the liver is mobilized towards the midline to expose the right adrenal gland. The vena cava is dissected off the adrenal gland by removing soft tissue attachments with lateral traction using robotic bipolar forceps. Hook cautery is employed to carefully take down the soft tissue attachments, avoiding thermal damage to surrounding tissues. The Girota fascia in contact with the lateral aspect of the tumor is dissected, revealing the capsule of the kidney posteriorly. Hemostasis is achieved with the hook cautery.

The surgeon dissects the adrenal gland circumferentially, isolating and clipping the right adrenal vein with a Hem-o-lok clip. Soft tissue attachments from the tumor to the lateral wall of the vena cava are carefully taken down. An extended vessel sealer is used for coagulation and dissection of the soft tissue around the tumor mass, providing exposure and lateral traction as the dissection progresses cephalad.

The robotic system is used to perform a clean dissection, while the assistant evacuates smoke from the vessel sealer to maintain a clear operative field. The final attachments are taken down with the vessel sealer, achieving hemostasis. The specimen is removed using a laparoscopic endobag, similar to the one used for cholecystectomy. The area is irrigated with chlorhexidine solution to clear minimal blood clots, and all port sites are closed under direct laparoscopic visualization.
Pathology showed metastatic malignant pleomorphic sarcoma with four centimeters in size with negative margins. The patient did very well and was discharged home on day two.
